description: inventa.ai CadQuery knowledge base — product dimensions, construction strategies, error patterns, and best practices for AI-generated CAD code.

box.notes: Only primitive that supports centered= kwarg. Use centered=(True,True,False) for main body so Z=0 is ground.

cylinder.notes: Use for motors, standoffs, round holes, dowels.

sphere.notes: Use for decorative caps, ball joints, domes.

polygon.notes: Regular polygon inscribed in circle. Use for hex nuts, knobs.

extrude.notes: DOES NOT support centered=. Never pass centered= to extrude.

revolve.notes: Profile must be entirely on ONE side of axis. Best for cups, bottles, vases, round products.

loft.notes: Connects multiple cross-sections. Sections must have compatible wire counts. Best for organic shapes, tapered bodies.

sweep.notes: Sweeps profile along path. Path must have no sharp corners — use .radiusArc() not .lineTo() for turns.

cutBlind.notes: Cut into face. Negative depth goes into material.

cut.notes: Subtracts geometry. Cutter must overlap body. Use wall*3 depth for through-cuts.

union.notes: Joins geometry. Parts MUST physically overlap by >= 0.5mm. Non-overlapping union creates disconnected solid.

fillet.notes: ALWAYS wrap in try/except. Guard: min(r, min(L,W,H)*0.25). Fillet BEFORE shell, not after.

chamfer.notes: Flat bevel. Safer than fillet for tiny edges. Wrap in try/except.

shell.notes: Hollows solid. Negative = inward. Apply fillets BEFORE shell. Must have adequate wall thickness.

ellipse.notes: For loft cross-sections on ergonomic products.

slot2D.notes: Stadium/pill shape. Use for ports, buttons, slots — NOT .box().

spline.notes: Smooth curve through points. Best for organic profiles (bottles, vases).

tangentArcPoint.notes: Arc tangent to previous segment. Perfect for lip/rim transitions.

workplane.notes: Creates new workplane offset from current.

faces.notes: Select faces by direction. >Z=top, <Z=bottom, >X=right, etc. May fail after booleans change topology.

translate.notes: Move solid. Compute positions FROM parent dimensions, never hardcode.

center.notes: Offset sketch origin on current workplane.

polarArray.notes: Circular pattern. Use for screw holes, vents.

hole.notes: Precision through-hole or blind hole. Use for screw holes, pin holes.

construction_strategies.name: box_plus_shell
construction_strategies.description: Start with .box() + .shell() to create a hollow enclosure, then .cut() features.
construction_strategies.pattern:
body = cq.Workplane('XY').box(L, W, H, centered=(True,True,False))
body = body.edges('|Z').fillet(corner_r)
body = body.faces('>Z').shell(-wall)

construction_strategies.name: revolve_profile
construction_strategies.description: Draw 2D profile on XZ plane, revolve 360° around Y axis for axially symmetric products.

construction_strategies.name: loft_sections
construction_strategies.description: Multiple cross-sections at different heights, lofted together for smooth organic transitions.
construction_strategies.pattern:
body = cq.Workplane('XY').ellipse(W*0.4, D*0.35)
  .workplane(offset=H*0.3).ellipse(W*0.5, D*0.45)
  .workplane(offset=H*0.4).ellipse(W*0.45, D*0.4)
  .workplane(offset=H*0.3).ellipse(W*0.3, D*0.25)
  .loft()

construction_strategies.name: swept_path
construction_strategies.description: Sweep a profile along a curved path. Path MUST use arcs, not sharp corners.
construction_strategies.pattern:
path = cq.Workplane('XZ').moveTo(0,0).radiusArc((L,0), radius)
profile = cq.Workplane('XY').circle(tube_r)
body = profile.sweep(path)

construction_strategies.name: additive_assembly
construction_strategies.description: Build separate components, position with .translate(), combine with .union(). Parts MUST overlap.
construction_strategies.pattern:
body = cq.Workplane('XY').box(L, W, H, centered=(True,True,False))
arm = cq.Workplane('XY').box(arm_l, arm_w, arm_h)
body = body.union(arm.translate((x, y, z)))

construction_strategies.name: profile_extrude
construction_strategies.description: Draw custom cross-section, extrude to depth.

construction_strategies.name: subtractive_sculpt
construction_strategies.description: Start with solid block, remove material with multiple .cut() operations.
construction_strategies.pattern:
body = cq.Workplane('XY').box(L, W, H, centered=(True,True,False))
cutter = cq.Workplane('XY').box(pocket_l, pocket_w, pocket_h)
body = body.cut(cutter.translate((x, y, z)))

construction_strategies.name: architectural
construction_strategies.description: Floor-by-floor construction with stacked boxes, roof geometry, window cutouts.
construction_strategies.pattern:
base = cq.Workplane('XY').box(L, W, floor_h, centered=(True,True,False))
for i in range(num_floors):
  floor = cq.Workplane('XY').box(L, W, floor_h).translate((0, 0, i*floor_h))
  base = base.union(floor)

gear.notes: Use cq_warehouse InvoluteGear if available, else approximate with polygon + extrude.

GEOMETRY_FILLET_CHAMFER.cause: Fillet radius too large for edge, or edge no longer exists after boolean cut.
GEOMETRY_FILLET_CHAMFER.fix_strategy: Reduce radius by 30-60%, wrap in try/except, move fillets before cuts.
GEOMETRY_FILLET_CHAMFER.prevention: Always use min(r, min(L,W,H)*0.25). Fillet edges BEFORE boolean operations.

GEOMETRY_SHELL.cause: Wall thickness too thin, or conflicting fillets create invalid shell geometry.
GEOMETRY_SHELL.fix_strategy: Apply fillets BEFORE shell. Increase wall thickness. Remove conflicting chamfers.
GEOMETRY_SHELL.prevention: Shell after all fillets. wall >= 1.5mm. No chamfers on shell faces.

SKETCH_NOT_CLOSED.cause: 2D profile is open — missing .close() or segments don't connect.
SKETCH_NOT_CLOSED.fix_strategy: Add .close() before .extrude()/.revolve(). Check all segments connect.

SELECTOR_FAILED.cause: Face/edge selector returns empty result after topology changed by boolean.
SELECTOR_FAILED.fix_strategy: Use safer selectors, reorder operations, simplify selector string.
SELECTOR_FAILED.prevention: Avoid selectors after complex booleans. Use .translate() positioning instead.

CURVE_TANGENT.cause: Degenerate spline/arc with coincident points or zero radius.
CURVE_TANGENT.fix_strategy: Replace spline with simpler arc/line. Ensure distinct control points.

REVOLVE_AXIS.cause: Profile crosses or touches the revolution axis.
REVOLVE_AXIS.fix_strategy: Ensure profile stays entirely on one side of axis (all X > 0 for Y-axis revolve).
REVOLVE_AXIS.prevention: Start profile at (small_offset, 0) not (0, 0) if using points near axis.

MATH_ERROR.cause: Division by zero, sqrt of negative, invalid parameter combinations.
MATH_ERROR.fix_strategy: Add guards: max(value, 0.1), use abs() for safety.
MATH_ERROR.prevention: Validate parameter ranges. Use min/max guards.

LOFT_FAILED.cause: Cross-sections have different wire counts or incompatible topology.
LOFT_FAILED.fix_strategy: Ensure all loft sections have same number of wires. Use simpler shapes.
LOFT_FAILED.prevention: Use same primitive type for all sections (all ellipses or all rects).

BOOLEAN_FAILED.cause: Cut/union operands don't overlap, or produce degenerate geometry.
BOOLEAN_FAILED.fix_strategy: Ensure overlap >= 0.5mm. Scale cutter slightly larger.
BOOLEAN_FAILED.prevention: Compute positions from parent dimensions. Verify overlap before .cut()/.union().

WRONG_PARAMETER.fix_strategy: Remove invalid kwargs. Only .box() supports centered=.

SWEEP_FAILED.cause: Profile can't follow path (sharp corners, self-intersection).
SWEEP_FAILED.fix_strategy: Replace sharp corners with .radiusArc(). Simplify path.
SWEEP_FAILED.prevention: Use smooth arcs in path. Profile must be smaller than path bend radius.

WORKPLANE_STACK.cause: Operations applied to wrong workplane in the stack.
WORKPLANE_STACK.fix_strategy: Use explicit .workplane() calls. Don't chain too deeply.
WORKPLANE_STACK.prevention: Create separate workplanes for each major feature.

OCC_KERNEL_ERROR.fix_strategy: Simplify geometry. Replace complex operations with primitives.
OCC_KERNEL_ERROR.prevention: Avoid very thin features (< 0.1mm). Ensure valid dimensions.

ATTRIBUTE_ERROR.cause: Calling method on None — selector returned no result.
ATTRIBUTE_ERROR.fix_strategy: Check if selector result exists before calling methods.
ATTRIBUTE_ERROR.prevention: Use safe selector patterns with fallback.

TYPE_ERROR.cause: Wrong argument type — int where float expected, etc.
TYPE_ERROR.fix_strategy: Ensure all dimensions are float (5.0 not 5).
TYPE_ERROR.prevention: Use float literals for all dimensions.

EMPTY_RESULT.fix_strategy: Verify operands overlap. Scale cutter to ensure intersection.
EMPTY_RESULT.prevention: Always check that cut doesn't remove entire body.

NAME_ERROR.cause: Undefined variable — typo or missing definition.
NAME_ERROR.fix_strategy: Define variable before use. Check spelling.
NAME_ERROR.prevention: Use consistent naming. Define ALL variables at top.

ASSEMBLY_ARRANGEMENT.cause: Parts added with .union() don't physically touch the main body.
ASSEMBLY_ARRANGEMENT.fix_strategy: Recompute .translate() positions from parent dimensions. Ensure overlap >= 0.5mm.
ASSEMBLY_ARRANGEMENT.prevention: Always compute: arm_top_z = body_z, motor_z = arm_z + arm_h. Never add gaps.

self_healing_phases.strategy: Fix only the crashing line, reduce radii by 30%.
Reduce radii 60%, wrap in try/except, simplify splines.
Strip all fillets/chamfers/shells, replace lofts with extrusions.
Rewrite the failing section using only safe primitives.
Rebuild entire model from scratch with boxes & cylinders only.

preprocessing_pipeline.name: strip_centered_from_non_box
preprocessing_pipeline.description: Remove centered= kwarg from .extrude(), .rect(), .circle() — only .box() supports it.

preprocessing_pipeline.name: ensure_box_grounding
preprocessing_pipeline.description: Add centered=(True,True,False) to first main .box() so Z=0 is ground.

preprocessing_pipeline.name: fix_zero_dimensions
preprocessing_pipeline.description: Replace .extrude(0) with .extrude(0.1) to prevent degenerate geometry.

preprocessing_pipeline.name: fix_negative_z_positions
preprocessing_pipeline.description: Warn about features placed below Z=0.

preprocessing_pipeline.name: ensure_result_assignment
preprocessing_pipeline.description: Auto-add 'result = body' if AI forgets to assign result variable.

preprocessing_pipeline.name: clamp_fillet_radii
preprocessing_pipeline.description: Inject min(r, _auto_fillet_max) guards — limits to 15% of smallest dimension.

preprocessing_pipeline.name: wrap_fillets_in_try_except
preprocessing_pipeline.description: Wrap unprotected .fillet()/.chamfer() calls in try/except blocks.

coordinate_system.Z: Always vertical/upward. Tallest dimension for upright products, thickness for flat products.
coordinate_system.origin: World center. Main body uses centered=(True,True,False) so bottom sits at Z=0.

positioning_rules: NEVER hardcode absolute positions — always compute from body dimensions.
Parts joined with .union() MUST overlap by >= 0.5mm.
Cutters must have depth >= wall*3 to guarantee through-cuts.
Features on surfaces: use face selectors or explicit z = body_z for top face.
Z=0 is ground plane. Everything should sit above it unless intentionally embedded.
Compute child positions FROM parent: motor_z = arm_z + arm_height, not motor_z = 45.

external_grip.description: Large fillets on grip areas for comfort.

decorative.description: Medium fillets on visible edges for smooth look.

opening_safety.description: Small fillets on port/opening edges for safety.

rubber_feet.description: 4+ small cylinders on bottom face for grip/stability.
rubber_feet.pattern:
foot = cq.Workplane('XY').cylinder(foot_h, foot_r)
body = body.union(foot.translate((x, y, 0)))

label_recess.description: Shallow rectangular recess on bottom for product label.

vent_grille.description: Array of slots or holes for ventilation.

screw_boss.description: Cylindrical post with hole for screw mounting.

pcb_standoff.description: Short cylindrical post to elevate PCB above enclosure floor.

grip_texture.description: Array of shallow grooves on grip surfaces.

panel_line.description: Shallow groove representing manufacturing parting line.

common_mistakes.mistake: Using .box() for port cutouts on electronics
common_mistakes.correct: Use .slot2D() for USB-C/HDMI (stadium shape), .cylinder() for speaker/mic holes.

common_mistakes.mistake: Fillet after boolean cut changes edge topology
common_mistakes.correct: Apply fillets BEFORE cuts, or use try/except with reduced radius fallback.
Only .box() supports centered=. Remove from all other operations.
Fillet ALL visible edges FIRST, then apply .shell().
Compute positions from parent dimensions. Ensure >= 0.5mm overlap.
Always compute: usb_y = -body_y/2 - 0.1, camera_z = body_z - camera_inset.
Keep all X coordinates > 0 for Y-axis revolve. Start at (small_offset, 0).
Use 5.0 not 5 for all dimensions to prevent type errors.
Every sketch chain needs .close() before .extrude() or .revolve().

common_mistakes.mistake: Loft sections with different wire counts
common_mistakes.correct: All cross-sections must use same primitive type (all ellipses, all rects).
Use .radiusArc() or .threePointArc() instead of .lineTo() for turns.

common_mistakes.mistake: Drone without visible motors and propellers
common_mistakes.correct: Every air drone MUST have cylinder motors + thin disc propellers on every arm tip.
Use real-world reference dimensions. A mug is 85mm wide, not 20mm.
Main body uses centered=(True,True,False) so Z starts at 0. All features Z >= 0.

minimum_features.description: Total boolean operations (cuts + unions) expected per complexity level.

minimum_parameters.description: At least 8 adjustable parameters with name, default, min, max, unit.

code_length.description: Minimum expected code lines per complexity level.

volume_sanity.description: Result must have positive volume. Zero/negative = degenerate geometry.

parametric_script_markers.warning: NEVER rename or reformat these markers — rebuild endpoint uses str.find() on these exact strings.

output_format.description: AI must return raw JSON (no markdown fences, no ```json wrapper).

schema.code:
import cadquery as cq
# ... CadQuery code ...
result = body

explanation.design_intent: Brief description of what was built

rules: Code MUST import cadquery as cq.
Code MUST define 'result' as a cq.Workplane solid.
All dimensions must use parameter variables, NOT hardcoded numbers.
Parameters must have realistic min/max ranges and 'mm' or 'degrees' units.
